post_rendered:
Mafia Game 302
Operation Toxikon

credits to NDMath for most of the OP and duke_of_wedgewood for flavor
  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • Day chat will be enabled via an AoPS private forum.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • Do not imitate the mods.
    • Confirm by counting the total number of bolded sections in this ruleset and answering it in your role PM, including any example bold votes but not the "Read all of the rules" phrase.
  • Voting/Elimination
    • This game will partially use the voting rules philosophy of "if it looks like a vote, it's a vote". So spelling/capitalization does not matter so long as the “Vote:” part is right, and the following two rules are met.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>", or c or code text: 
      Vote: <insert player here>
      .
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • You may vote for no elimination to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Elimination" or: 
      Vote: No Elimination
      .
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though you have the ability to do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote" or: 
      Unvote
      . An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • If the deadline is met the player with the most votes is eliminated. If there is a tie, the player who received the votes first is eliminated. If No Elimination is part of the tie, No Elimination occurs.
    • Starting day 2, If more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be eliminated. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • Starting day 2, If at least half of the votes are for no elimination, then there will be no elimination. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, it will be twilight and no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I (or the comod) post a end-of-day scene, the person eliminated (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
    • The day action deadline will occur 48 hours before the day deadline; in other words, day vigilante shots cannot be used within 48 hours of the day deadline. If one wishes to use a day vigilante device on any certain day, they should make sure to submit their action before this deadline.
    • The night deadline will be 48 hours after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • If a player does not make 3 game-advancing posts in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player receives two consecutive PM prods or accumulates a total of 4 PM prods, they will be replaced, unless they make a number of posts deemed sufficient by the moderator to avoid replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, they must PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language or make personal attacks.
    • Be mature.
    • The game is to not be considered over until the mod declares it as such.
    • Don't intentionally/purposely gamethrow. If you believe you can no longer win talk to me in your role pm.
    • For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, they may be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators
    • I (scannose) am the moderator of Mafia Game 302. duke_of_wedgewood is the co-moderator.
    • This turquoise-ish color (#76a5af) is my mod color. Do not post in anything too similar.
    • The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game uses the BML10 setup, an open setup invented by BigMenLover9923. This setup consists of the following roles:

7 Vanilla Townies
Town 2-shot Dayvig Inventor
Mafia Goon
Mafia 2-shot Roleblocker

example role pms
vanilla townie wrote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 302.

You are a crew member (Vanilla Townie). You have no other power other than your voice and your vote.

You win when all members of the robots (Mafia) are eliminated. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
inventor wrote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 302.

You are a scientist (Town 2-shot Day Vigilante Inventor). Each night, you may choose to give another player a day vigilante device, which allows them to eliminate another player during daytime; they will receive the device by the beginning of the next day. You have two devices before you run out, and you may not give both devices to the same player.

You win when all members of the robots (Mafia) are eliminated. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
mafia goon wrote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 302.

You are an assistant engineer (Mafia Goon). You share a factional nightkill with your partner player, ship keyholder (Mafia 2-shot Roleblocker), and a factional private chat which the link to will be in the next message.

You win when your faction reaches parity with humans (Town). Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
mafia roleblocker wrote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 302.

You are the ship keyholder (Mafia 2-shot Roleblocker), and you also have tranquilizers for no particular reason whatsoever. Each night, you may choose to tranquilize a player and prevent them from potentially giving out any devices on that night. You only have two tranquilizing syringes before you run out, and you may not tranquilize the same player twice. In addition, you share a factional nightkill with your assistant engineer (Mafia Goon), player, and a factional private chat which the link to will be in the next message.

You win when your faction reaches parity with humans (Town). Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
gun message wrote:
You have received a 1-shot day vigilante device.

During any day and at least 48 hours before the day deadline, you may ask me in your role PM to shoot another player; that player will die instantly, and their role will be revealed. The device will be consumed upon use. The device will not reveal your identity, and its usage will not cause the current day to end (unless it causes one faction to achieve its win condition immediately).


Alive (2)
2. justJen
6. saturnrocket

Dead (8)
7. BananaBall00 K124659, crew member (Vanilla Townie)
8. sdmd nsd08 Bob_the_Bob, crew member (Vanilla Townie)
9. AwesomeKittyCat, crew member (Vanilla Townie)
3. Aminecraftbear BobDBuilder321, crew member (Vanilla Townie)
10. ad333, assistant engineer (Mafia Goon)
4. mhgelgi NDMath, crew member (Vanilla Townie)
1. NoSignOfTheta, scientist (Town 2-shot Day Vigilante Inventor)
5. cowbunga awesomeming327., crew member (Vanilla Townie)


Notable Stuff
nsd08 replaces sdmd
K124659 replaces BananaBall00
awesomeming327. replaces cowbunga
Bob_the_Bob replaces nsd08
Day 1 ends; Night 1 begins
Night 1 ends; Day 2 begins
BobDBuilder321 replaces Aminecraftbear
NDMath replaces mhgelgi
Day 2 ends; Night 2 begins
Night 2 ends; Day 3 begins
ad333 was dayvigged
Day 3 ends; Night 3 begins
Night 3 ends; Day 4 begins


Do not post until this line is crossed out.
Current state: game end

post_rendered:
Few visitors ever come to the End Island Research Center. It is very remote, sitting on the other side of the planet from any of the major cities. To travel to it you have to cross the dangerous rocky landscape that characterizes the planet. It's a location perfect for the islands purpose, Operation Toxikon.

It's a big day, the day part of the crew finally leaves the island, even though the weather doesn’t seem to agree. Gray clouds dump torrents of rain, pushed to the side but the wind. Lightning flashes over the ocean in the distance.

Out in the storm two drones float perfectly up in the clouds, far above the ocean. The pair starts flying swiftly straight towards the island. They stop directly above the busiest part of the island, where all the commotion is. A tiny camera silently emerges from one of the robots carapace.

Down below workers in stark blue uniforms soaked with water bustle around a massive hunk of metal, a great ship. Some push carts of supplies or connect fuel pipes, others call out orders. The great ship they’re centered around is in the shape of a circle, with massive engines below in the back and a shiny white and orange cockpit sitting above. Stamped onto its top is a symbol of an arrow jabbing into a tentacled robot. And on its hull stamped in massive Orange lettering are the words PSS Arcus.

Outside the main ship docking area bags of luggage glide up on an escalator coming from the residential buildings below.

Suddenly the robots both turn that direction and dive.

Confirm Count 1.1 - yes there is a confirm rule

Confirmed (2): ad333, Aminecraftbear
Unconfirmed (8): everyone else

post_rendered:
okay so setup-related things to start, having played a couple of games of this setup on MU:
(ad correct me if i'm wrong here)

this is not applicable to today!!

the general protocol is that if you get a dayvig, you should claim the dayvig asap. as far as i know, there isn't any disadvantage to this, and the point is that:

- if someone claims the dayvig, then they will be held responsible for the shot. this helps prevents wolves from making a shot on a PR (power role) / a towny player as this will put them into suspicion instantly. so, if the dayvig giver gives the dayvig to a wolf and that wolf claims the dayvig, then they will most likely make a less suspicious shot (i.e. on a townie that isn't cleared / very towny)
- if someone doesn't claim the dayvig, but it goes off, wolves probably have the dayvig. in which case, if the dayvig giver is still alive, they should claim who they gave the dayvig to which automatically outs the wolf (if the wolves are smart they'll vig the dayvig giver which is why you shouldn't out. more on that)
- for the above reasons it is important to claim the dayvig if you're town and you have it ASAP!
if you're town and you make an unclaimed shot on town, and you get hanged after that, it's on you

as with other games, the dayvig giver should not be claiming. the rationale of all other games applies: not only can wolves nk you, they can also rb you. the case in which you should be claiming (along with your actions) is when there is an unclaimed shot on a consensus townie then you have reason to be suspicious that you gave your gun to a wolf. aside from that, claiming might be appropriate after you have given your guns (d3) for confirming yourself and making town's job easier.
post_canonical:
okay so setup-related things to start, having played a couple of games of this setup on MU:
(ad correct me if i'm wrong here)

this is not applicable to today!!

the general protocol is that if you get a dayvig, you should claim the dayvig asap. as far as i know, there isn't any disadvantage to this, and the point is that:

- if someone claims the dayvig, then they will be held responsible for the shot. this helps prevents wolves from making a shot on a PR (power role) / a towny player as this will put them into suspicion instantly. so, if the dayvig giver gives the dayvig to a wolf and that wolf claims the dayvig, then they will most likely make a less suspicious shot (i.e. on a townie that isn't cleared / very towny)
- if someone doesn't claim the dayvig, but it goes off, wolves probably have the dayvig. in which case, if the dayvig giver is still alive, they should [b]claim who they gave the dayvig to which automatically outs the wolf[/b] (if the wolves are smart they'll vig the dayvig giver which is why you shouldn't out. more on that)
- [b]for the above reasons it is important to claim the dayvig if you're town and you have it ASAP![/b]
if you're town and you make an unclaimed shot on town, and you get hanged after that, it's on you 

as with other games, the dayvig giver should not be claiming. the rationale of all other games applies: not only can wolves nk you, they can also rb you. the case in which you should be claiming (along with your actions) is when there is an unclaimed shot on a consensus townie then you have reason to be suspicious that you gave your gun to a wolf. aside from that, claiming might be appropriate after you have given your guns (d3) for confirming yourself and making town's job easier.

saturnrocket wrote:
okay so setup-related things to start, having played a couple of games of this setup on MU:
(ad correct me if i'm wrong here)

this is not applicable to today!!

the general protocol is that if you get a dayvig, you should claim the dayvig asap. as far as i know, there isn't any disadvantage to this, and the point is that:

- if someone claims the dayvig, then they will be held responsible for the shot. this helps prevents wolves from making a shot on a PR (power role) / a towny player as this will put them into suspicion instantly. so, if the dayvig giver gives the dayvig to a wolf and that wolf claims the dayvig, then they will most likely make a less suspicious shot (i.e. on a townie that isn't cleared / very towny)
- if someone doesn't claim the dayvig, but it goes off, wolves probably have the dayvig. in which case, if the dayvig giver is still alive, they should claim who they gave the dayvig to which automatically outs the wolf (if the wolves are smart they'll vig the dayvig giver which is why you shouldn't out. more on that)
- for the above reasons it is important to claim the dayvig if you're town and you have it ASAP!
if you're town and you make an unclaimed shot on town, and you get hanged after that, it's on you

as with other games, the dayvig giver should not be claiming. the rationale of all other games applies: not only can wolves nk you, they can also rb you. the case in which you should be claiming (along with your actions) is when there is an unclaimed shot on a consensus townie then you have reason to be suspicious that you gave your gun to a wolf. aside from that, claiming might be appropriate after you have given your guns (d3) for confirming yourself and making town's job easier.

low-key i haven't really played this setup a lot but yeah. Anyhow slank cover d1 & probably d2, you would probably give it to me anyways since my definition of slank cover is still more active than most people but here are my mech thoughts

1) claim if & only if you are in direct danger of being hanged (or if you've already given both dayvigs in which case it's probably LYLO so yeah go ahead)
2) just don't FPS (claim being the inventor if you're not)
3) if you recieve the dayvig you might as well claim it? I'm admittedly not very familiar with the setup but that's typically the play as far as i know. It doesn't seem entirely necessary particularly if the inventor is still alive but
3.5) Don't be stupid, if everybody is saying that somebody is town just don't shoot them. This is maybe potentially negotiable with people in thread but generally just don't
4) don't holster it's cringe (maybe niche cases in which it's worth it but)

theta's first post is the only actually interesting post

post_rendered:
Read of the day (2 of 2 for activity reasons): Aminecraftbear

Somehow, despite his large volume of posts, Aminecraftbear and his posts have left almost no impression on me. As a member of the Mafia faction in 293 and 296, this is one of the things that people mentioned in an attempt to case me. However, they were not able to form a logical argument so that ultimately materialized to nothing. But this time seems to be a bit different, because each of his posts do ping me in some sort of way (either towny or wolfy) and it isn't the same as me, where none of my posts are pingy as a wolf. But maybe that is just a difference in our metas.

Towny posts: #50, #63

Wolfy posts: #54, #55, #58, #60

Bad push on bb00, bad response to ad
The only thing going for him is his activity, and some of his self-consciousness. The stuff where he outright says things like "... I am town ..." is annoying but at the same time, good for him, because someone who randed mafia after a long time would be more careful.
You could consider this to be partially an activity read, but maybe I was too harsh in reading his posts in the #50 - #60 range. Maybe he just forgot how to play the game and wants towncred fast, but anyhow, I don't see his activity pointing him towards being a wolf. This goes hand-in-hand with fluff, because while he is fluffing a little bit, it wasn't hard for me to find his content posts, and it seems like he has a "serious" lever that he can turn on at any time, kind of like K12, which is good. One other thing that I don't like is his wallsitting, which is pretty prominent in his posts. For those who don't know, wallsitting is when you don't give a strong opinion or read on a player, and kind of just throw shade and try to push them in a hesitated way, in order to avoid backlash and questioning from others.

(New update) Posts regarding me:
#83 - You may be right in that I my expectation for newbies to reach a townlean or townread position may be pretty low, but cowbunga belongs here, especially now that I have seen the behavior of other newbies in this game. Even if he has randed mafia with a less experienced player or one who hasn't seen either the main thread or the mafia thread first, I do not see him frame 1 attacking you. It's one of the crazy reads (not a tinfoil because you aren't consensus town) that actually generated a good bit of discussion. I can't really defend them further because they haven't exactly posted much since my RoTD1/2, but my points there still apply.

Read of the day (2 of 2 for activity reasons): Aminecraftbear

Somehow, despite his large volume of posts, Aminecraftbear and his posts have left almost no impression on me. As a member of the Mafia faction in 293 and 296, this is one of the things that people mentioned in an attempt to case me. But this time seems to be a bit different, because each of his posts do ping me in some sort of way (either towny or wolfy) and it isn't the same as me, where none of my posts are pingy as a wolf. But maybe that is just a difference in our metas.

Towny posts: #50, #63

Wolfy posts: #54, #55, #58, #60

Bad push on bb00, bad response to ad
The only thing going for him is his activity, and some of his self-consciousness. The stuff where he outright says things like "... I am town ..." is annoying but at the same time, good for him, because someone who randed mafia after a long time would be more careful.
You could consider this to be partially an activity read, but maybe I was too harsh in reading his posts in the #50 - #60 range. Maybe he just forgot how to play the game and wants towncred fast, but anyhow, I don't see his activity pointing him towards being a wolf. This goes hand-in-hand with fluff, because while he is fluffing a little bit, it wasn't hard for me to find his content posts, and it seems like he has a "serious" lever that he can turn on at any time, kind of like K12, which is good. One other thing that I don't like is his wallsitting, which is pretty prominent in his posts. For those who don't know, wallsitting is when you don't give a strong opinion or read on a player, and kind of just throw shade and try to push them in a hesitated way, in order to avoid backlash and questioning from others.

It's one of the crazy reads (not a tinfoil because you aren't consensus town) that actually generated a good bit of discussion. I can't really defend them further because they haven't exactly posted much since my RoTD1/2, but my points there still apply.

So I read some of this game and I think I saw theta giving out reads of the days, except he's like microreading while I think I've come up with a breakthrough in flavor solving. I think I can figure out what the setup is through scan's discreet hints throughout the flavor.
scannose wrote:
Few visitors ever come to the End Island Research Center. It is very remote, sitting on the other side of the planet from any of the major cities. To travel to it you have to cross the dangerous rocky landscape that characterizes the planet. It's a location perfect for the islands purpose, Operation Toxikon.

It's a big day, the day part of the crew finally leaves the island, even though the weather doesn’t seem to agree. Gray clouds dump torrents of rain, pushed to the side but the wind. Lightning flashes over the ocean in the distance.

Out in the storm two drones float perfectly up in the clouds, far above the ocean. The pair starts flying swiftly straight towards the island. They stop directly above the busiest part of the island, where all the commotion is. A tiny camera silently emerges from one of the robots carapace. theta is unemployed

Down below workers in stark blue uniforms soaked with water bustle around a massive hunk of metal, a great ship. Some push carts of supplies or connect fuel pipes, others call out orders. The great ship they’re centered around is in the shape of a circle, with massive engines below in the back and a shiny white and orange cockpit sitting above. Stamped onto its top is a symbol of an arrow jabbing into a tentacled robot. And on its hull stamped in massive Orange lettering are the words PSS Arcus.

Outside the main ship docking area bags of luggage glide up on an escalator coming from the residential buildings below.

Suddenly the robots both turn that direction and dive.

additional tips for dayvig giver and future tips
saturnrocket wrote:
okay so setup-related things to start, having played a couple of games of this setup on MU:
(ad correct me if i'm wrong here)

this is not applicable to today!!

the general protocol is that if you get a dayvig, you should claim the dayvig asap. as far as i know, there isn't any disadvantage to this, and the point is that:

- if someone claims the dayvig, then they will be held responsible for the shot. this helps prevents wolves from making a shot on a PR (power role) / a towny player as this will put them into suspicion instantly. so, if the dayvig giver gives the dayvig to a wolf and that wolf claims the dayvig, then they will most likely make a less suspicious shot (i.e. on a townie that isn't cleared / very towny)
- if someone doesn't claim the dayvig, but it goes off, wolves probably have the dayvig. in which case, if the dayvig giver is still alive, they should claim who they gave the dayvig to which automatically outs the wolf (if the wolves are smart they'll vig the dayvig giver which is why you shouldn't out. more on that)
- for the above reasons it is important to claim the dayvig if you're town and you have it ASAP!
if you're town and you make an unclaimed shot on town, and you get hanged after that, it's on you

as with other games, the dayvig giver should not be claiming. the rationale of all other games applies: not only can wolves nk you, they can also rb you. the case in which you should be claiming (along with your actions) is when there is an unclaimed shot on a consensus townie then you have reason to be suspicious that you gave your gun to a wolf. aside from that, claiming might be appropriate after you have given your guns (d3) for confirming yourself and making town's job easier.

post_rendered:
Dr. Erasmus Koro stares at the 3 shiny black guns in front of him. To the government these weapons are MDA-3s but to him they’re Toxikons. These are the weapons that will finally be able to destroy thinking machines in an instant, the poisoned arrow they'll die with. They're his life's work, and the purpose of Operation Toxikon.

There’s nothing he can do at this point. After years of working and billions in funding they’re done, well basically. This is his last look at them before they’ll be launched into space and brought to the assembly against machines.

An announcement sounds over the loudspeakers. All ship crew members come to the Arcus and prepare for departure. All crew members report to the ship.

A tall skinny young man drags his luggage, the wheels grinding against the metal floor of the spaceship hallway. Beside him stands a shorter man, neatly dressed with nicely combed hair.
Eventually they stop at one of the small metal doors flush with the wall. The tall man pulls out a key card and opens the door with a metallic click. The inside of the room is very stark, simple and functional. A double bunk bed. Two tables. Two chairs.
The luggage is left heaped on the floor.
A voice is heard throughout the ship Prepare for launch. All crew members prepare for lift off.
The two of them go over and lay on the beds, strapping themselves in. Sounds of moving machinery sound from all around and the room begins to slowly rotate. The beds and the tables rotate seemlessly along with the turning room.
The luggage slides across the room and slams into the wall.
Outside are sounds of final metal machinery clicking into place and the engine beginning to roar. All of a sudden the engine bursts with force and the whole ship lifts into sky, pushing them into the newfound floor.
Across the room the suitcases suddenly pry open and a metal shapes bursts out. The men watch with fear as two shapes of metal tentacles and iron eyes fly towards them. In a few seconds, they’re dead.
The next moment the robots each inject themselves into the bodies, from which rise new men, fake men, robot men.

Now comes easiest part of the ride, floating off into the void, the captain thinks. He calmly sits in the cockpit gazing off at the distance stars. He can just see the distant planet that they're going to.
A ding comes from his computer, it's a transmission from the planet below. His expression quickly turns from contentment to concern, and then terror.
An intelligent machine threat is confirmed to have successfully boarded the ship. Eliminating the threat at all costs is off the highest priority. They are probably after the guns.
A moment later a shorter message comes, this time from the inventor.
One of the guns is missing.

An announcement blasts over the speakers. All crew members come to the main hall.
Everyone gathers in the hall, nervous about what they had heard had happened. They restlessly shuffle around the room with nervous whispers. The voice comes from the speakers stationed around the room again.
The ship has been infiltrated by a machine perpetrator. We must find the compromised individual at all costs, for the success of the mission, even if it results in the death of innocent comrades. Are best option is to use are greatest asset, human deduction. By tonight you must have collectively chosen someone who you think is the perpetrator. Good Luck.
The crew members are even more nervous now, they have to potentially eliminate one of their one. Chatter echoes throughout the room as discussion begins. Throughout the day discussion continues, and many candidates are proposed. In the end most of the crew members settle on two possible candidates. Is it awesomeming or K124659? The amount of supporters for each candidate is close.
Eventually when the massive clock in the central hall finally announces the arrival of night time the crew members final consensus is set in stone. But were they right?
The crew has reached a conclusion. K124659 will be taken away to be executed.
The crew watched solemnly as K124659 is escorted out of the room. They quietly listen as he is shot in the adjacent room, his final scream echoing through the ship. His body is opened. But there is no robot infiltration to be found inside.

K124659 was a flip

The next morning the crew members each woke up in their individual rooms. They were emotionally shaken from what happened the previous night, the death of one of their own. But the difficulties weren't over yet. They eat their breakfast, waiting for what will happen in the day to come.

post_rendered:
At 5:45 am sharp, ship time, the captain's alarm clock rings, synchronous with many others across the ship. He sleepily climbs out of his bunk and walks over to his coffee maker. A few minutes later he's got his uniform on and a nice warm cup of the stuff that keeps him running, coffee. The ship commander walks through the grey hall and then up the ladder to the cockpit.
"Hi"
"That was a long night shift." his assistant pilot says. He can see the bags under the man's eyes.
"Gonna be a long day of ahead us again probably." the captain responds. Could this man be one of those infected by a robot? Could he be talking to a robot right now? It's hard to believe, but it's very possible in this situation.
The captain sit's down in the first officers seat. He looks around at the innumerable controls familiar to him. Thankfully there isn't much to control right now.
The secondary pilot walks down the hall, back towards his room, hoping for some rest. But rest will have to wait, an announcement blasts over the speakers. All available crew members report to the main hall.
Dr. Erasmus Koro turns to talk to the few crew members in front of him. He cleared his throat and the side conversations stop.
"The death of our two comrades is very sad but we can't let it turn us away from the mission. Today we'll continue to use conversation and human deduction, I still think that's are best weapon. I also have 2 toxikon guns still available to me, which we can use to our advantage. But it's best for the perpetrators not to know who has them for now. Let's hope we have better luck this day."
Conversation starts again but they're forced to break up. The crew members still have responsibilities to fill and a need to continue with their life. Some operate the engines, some wash clothes, others calculate their trajectory or check on the life support systems while others simply eat breakfast.
Though still throughout the day discussion continues and accusations are thrown. Many targets are proposed.
"I think it's theta" one person says.
"Not its ad and ming, theta is innocent"
"How am I suspicious?"
And throughout the day votes are cast. Even though there are different ideas there's also consensus, a consensus that only grows stronger as the day grows longer.
"Kitty is the perpetrator, we should vote her."
"Yeah I agree, she seems suspicious."
When night comes they all come back together in the main hall, eager to hear the results of their discussions. Everyone watches the big screen for the last time that day as the ships operating system calculates the votes. The loud speakers announce the victim of tonights execution for all to hear. AwesomeKittyCat has been chosen to be executed.
The room is solemnly silent as she is dragged away to be executed. The crew braces as they hear a shot from the other room, another one of their comrades, or a robot, dead by their own hands. The corpse is searched, and AwesomeKittyCat is quickly identified as a flip
scannose wrote:
Welcome, AwesomeKittyCat, to Mafia Game 302.

You are a crew member (Vanilla Townie). You have no power other than your voice and your vote.

You win when all members of the robots (Mafia) are eliminated. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

The body is carried to the airlock and ejected out into space, another comrade lost.
"Only 7 of us now."
.
It is now Night 2. The deadline for Night 2 is May 9, 3pm est.

Throughout the day the remaining crew members continue to discuss who it could be. Suspicions are raised and thoughts are shared but nothing is concluded yet. No shots are given yet, only harsh accusations.
Even with these important evaluations going on they still have to run their ship. The inventor documents his discoveries. Workers operate the engines. The Captain directs the ship. Guards protect the weapons. People sit in the mess hall eating their lunch and talking with one another. There some of the crew members try to lighten the mood by talking about happy things like their distant families at home and good movies. But the death, the stress and the accusations still sit there, an elephant in the room.
Suddenly the sound of a shot rings throughout the ship.
Crew members run from the mess hall towards the sound of the shot, down the long metallic halls till eventually they find the sight of the shot. More people come from the other direction, panting.
In front of them all lying in the middle of the corridor is a body. There's a blackened wound from the shot of a toxikon gun in the chest. They recognize the body as that of someone who they once respected as an honest comrade, ad333. They investigate the body further and find that they were a flip.

general reasoning for why I think it's saturn
Play Patterns
When playing as wolf with them in 297, I discovered saturn and I like to take a lot of the same positions as scum.
ie.
>Be off-wagon at least once but then don't be afraid to push eliminations through.
>Buss frequently when there's weakness but bussing won't send them over
>Try to fall into line with the most vocal players & their world view

Game State
The game state suggests to me that town has not been right all game (up until today). And with Ming & Jen as major d1 wagons while saturn has faced comparatively little pressure, this is pretty relevant.

There's a couple major reasons I feel the gamestate suggests this;
1. Only town flips
~This one's pretty obvious as to why it would suggest town hasn't been right. The games where town is never right early on tend to be wolf sweeps.
2. Stagnation
~The top wagons have tended to be pretty stagnant throughout the day (for day2 at least, but I would be surprised if this didn't apply to day1 also) which suggests that wolves are happy with the gamestate. If wolves were not happy, there would be more attempt at shakeups; Town would not have put Kitty to L-1 three separate times yesterday.
~Also a critical indicator of this stagnation is the ad333 wagon that was attempted to be formed yesterday. It got two confident votes, from Theta and Bob, and then stalled out, a characteristic that as far as I'm aware has not applied to any of the other notable wagons this game.
3. Funneled Votes
~Do you know how hard it is to get four players to agree on the top choice for an elimination? Why do we actually have all the votes split on two player EoD1? That is just not a natural voting split for a deadline elimination game.
4. VT Claims
~Doesn't quite fit this section but feels worth pointing out; Scum under pressure early are more likely to claim pr if they claim at all, whereas most town are just vts

Process of Elimination
theta is inventor.
ming has checked all boxes for my expectations, his approach holding vigshot felt natural, & could be a pocket but him towning me is counter-productive for him as wolf. I also just don't see him almost dying like that d1 as wolf & being the very last vote to save himself, as well as I'd expect his motivation level to have been a little more synced with ad's if they were partners.
I don't really agree with any of the criticisms brought towards Jen, I think the vt claim was good from them and that they have had a number of solid posts throughout the game. Their takes are also more developed/nuanced than I'd expect them to have as wolf.
And then I'm town; I think ad's interaction with me would be a pretty dumb approach as w/w. I was intending to do some analysis on mhgelgi's posts but I think this was a more productive use of my time.

>Try to fall into line with the most vocal players & their world view
[/hide]
[hide=Game State]
The game state suggests to me that town has not been right all game (up until today). And with Ming & Jen as major d1 wagons while saturn has faced comparatively little pressure, this is pretty relevant.

There's a couple major reasons I feel the gamestate suggests this;
1. Only town flips
~This one's pretty obvious as to why it would suggest town hasn't been right. The games where town is never right early on tend to be wolf sweeps.
2. Stagnation
~The top wagons have tended to be pretty stagnant throughout the day (for day2 at least, but I would be surprised if this didn't apply to day1 also) which suggests that wolves are happy with the gamestate. If wolves were not happy, there would be more attempt at shakeups; Town would not have put Kitty to L-1 three separate times yesterday.
~Also a critical indicator of this stagnation is the ad333 wagon that was attempted to be formed yesterday. It got two confident votes, from Theta and Bob, and then stalled out, a characteristic that as far as I'm aware has not applied to any of the other notable wagons this game.
3. Funneled Votes
~Do you know how hard it is to get four players to agree on the top choice for an elimination? Why do we actually have all the votes split on two player EoD1? That is just not a natural voting split for a deadline elimination game.
4. VT Claims
~Doesn't quite fit this section but feels worth pointing out; Scum under pressure early are more likely to claim pr if they claim at all, whereas most town are just vts
[/hide]
[hide=Process of Elimination]

Here's my towncase for myself wrt f5:

I was towncore-ish for a large chunk of the day, which is evident from others' thoughts. This is mostly due to the actual conftown (theta) saying that I'm town (which reversed halfway through the day for reasons I'm still in the dark about): even if others don't necessarily agree, theta's and my thread presence is enough to make it the public opinion

I kind of didn't agree with why he townread me so strongly, but I kept quiet about it (which is NAI btw)

What did I do with this towncore position?

you can see for yourself, starting at 1510-ish I have actually been looking at the past posts and trying to figure something out. eventually i come to a conclusion and decide to push jen, but under pressure, jen "townslipped" more or less and then the thread was chaos after that

why my f5 makes sense as town
I don't trust anyone other than theta and myself. so as the towncore I would want to be the person "figuring things out" and posting my thoughts and stuff. i fully expected the towncore position to be permanent but i was wrong, so things changed drastically and i had to approach the game differently after that


more importantly, why it doesn't make sense as scum
i know you don't believe in self scum meta stuff, but I need to put this out there anyway, and you can make your own conclusions:
If I was scum, would I be more or less paranoid of my towncore position and more worried about whether it will change?
how would that impact my behavior?
If I was scum, why would I feel the need to go through the past posts and change my opinion on almost every single person i read throughout the day?
Do I seem more like a player who chooses the path of least resistance when I can?
Read my early f5 posts. do they seem like genuinely solving or are they performative?


Speaking completely neutrally, there's a lot of arguments to go against me, but i believe my f5 is strong.
Assuming you're town, i hope you figure it out. gl either way.
